sizing_rule.note: Ralph Vince optimal f method: find f value (0–1) that maximizes geometric mean using worst-case P&L from historical trades. Position quantity = (Account × optimal_f) / |worst_case_PnL|. Implementation: iterate f from 0 to 1 or use parabolic interpolation.

source_rule_text: Position size is determined by finding the optimal f value (between 0 and 1) that produces the highest geometric mean across historical trades, using the worst-case P&L to calculate position quantities.
